devnotes.summary: defines some base classes for 2 dimensional water and nitrate transport equations
largest index, required for the vectors during the flux calculation
Color array for concentration display
Problem: the dynamic implementation was removed again due to difficulties with array boundaries.
Problem: the dynamic implementation was removed again due to difficulties with array boundaries.

Declaration of class TRasterData. This class encapsulates the raster, positions and the number of read and randomized data (WAP). It enables display in the tabs RasterData and RootDistribution. The class also handles file access.
Description of the data set
Date [TDateTime format]
Number of rows
Number of columns
Number of roots
Height of rows [cm]
Width of columns [cm]
Root numbers in the raster (computational) cells. There can be more than one root per cell The array is needed for reading and displaying the number of roots in grid cells
Stores information on all roots in the form of TRootObject entries
Private declarations
Protected declarations
Reference to the diffusion submodel the raster data object belongs to
Implementation of the methods of TRasterData
Reads aggregated raster data (root counts) which are then used to randomly determine coordinate points. Example of functionality: readln(f, Ncols) assigns the value read from f to the variable Ncols.
Reads XY coordinates from a file and successively fills the passed (call-by-reference) PointSeries object with the XY pairs.
After reading aggregated root data, i.e. without exact coordinates, saves the generated random root coordinates (considering assignment to a 5x5 cm grid cell) as floating point numbers in a file.

class.name: TBaseSubmodRootDiff
class.devnotes: Published declarations Clears the Pos array members procedure ErasePosList; Declaration of class TBaseSubmodRootDiff. Base class for derived 2d and 1d diffusion models applied to a 2D area Functionality of the base class: all models can read raster data, exclude margins from calculations and display the data in the appropriate tabs of the Hume form. The 1D models does not output in the 3D plot tab. derived model simulate 2D water and nitrogen transport or apply for comparison 1D approaches to the same problem

devnotes.summary: a private field for showing the concentration field on a TMathimage object
Reference to the chart in the Hume form for displaying the root distribution
the codings for the concentration in colours
Reference to the AdvStringGrid in the Hume form for displaying raster data
Reference to the structural root model
Protected declarations
contains the x and y coordinates of the center of the container in container mode
sum of internal time steps

Field stores whether the output file for sink influx per cell has been created. This should be done anew before each model run -> SinkCellFileWasCreated is set to false in init. In CalcRates the file is created once and the flag is set to true

Field stores whether the output file for sink influx has been created. This should be done anew before each model run -> FileWasCreated is set to false in init. In CalcRates the file is created once and the flag is set to true
volume of a computational element [cm3]
water amount of a computational element [cm3]
minimum concentration in the grid [mol/cm3]
maximum concentration in the grid

VWC_xy: array for volumetric water content in the computational elements. WC_xy was declared as a dynamic array (see NG, p.65 ff) because the size of the array should be declared with values of dim_x and dim_y (number of computational elements in the x and y directions) that are assigned later.
In x_arr and y_arr are the 'midpoint coordinates of the grid cells'
text file for writing xy data
Object for displaying the root intersection points in the Hume form TChart object
Area of the whole soil section of interest [cm2] is handled as a constant
RasterData instance owned by the submodel
Indicates whether initialization has already occurred
Width of the computational domain [cm]
Height of the computational domain [cm]
Width of the raster grid (aggregated data) in x-direction [cm]
Width of the raster grid (aggregated data) in y-direction [cm]
number of elements in X-direction
number of elements in Y-direction
Maximum time step width [d]
Initial volumetric water content [cm3/cm3]
radius of the container in container mode
Depth of the layer [cm], assumption for mineralization calculation
devnotes.remarks: Mineralization model not yet available but should be implemented for both models
Margins are necessary so that edge effects can be excluded when the root exit points are hexagonally distributed
devnotes.summary: Vertical margin [cm]
Vertical margin [cm]
Horizontal margin [cm]
Depth at which evaluation should begin (in the 2D cross-section) [cm]
Thickness of the layer [cm]
Mean root length density [cm/ccm]
average Radius of the roots [cm]
grid width in X-direction [cm]
grid width in Y-direction [cm]
Mean root length density in a layer [cm/cm^3] calculated without roots located in the margins
Central area without margins [cm2]
Number of roots in center and margins [n]
Number of roots not located in margins []
Volume of the soil layer including margins [cm3]
actual volumetric water content [cm3/cm3]
Measure of error for regular distribution = number of roots that do not fit into the observation window when generating the uniform distribution, as a percentage of all roots [%%]
internal time step [d]
root length per hectar [cm/ha]
root length per hectar
Amount of water in the object [cm3]
Average distance between roots [cm]
Number of elements in the middle area in X-direction
Number of elements in the middle area in Y-direction
Option for Type of initialization, e.g. file or structural model
Path and name of the init file with XY root data
Path and name of the init file with root data as counts in a grid
Specify whether XY data should be written to a file
Path and name of the output file for XY data

Option for the type of root distribution distinct from the 1D model Options are 'Random', i.e. a random distribution of root exit points 'Regular', i.e. a regular grid distribution 'FromSource', i.e. a distribution based on source data
Both models can display roots in the RootDistribution tab.
Both models can write aggregated root data to the form. The method fills the aggregated root data (number of roots per grid cell) into the AdvStringGrid of the Hume form.

All diffusion models can compute a uniform distribution from a given one. The method distributes points based on a given WLD evenly over an area. The TPointSeries object of the calling HUME GUI method is refilled so the display can be updated. Different methods were created for this purpose and can be switched here.
Distribution using hexagons. Rows are filled sequentially. For better distribution the points still need to be shifted by half the radius of the circumscribed circle.

Distribution using hexagons. Columns are filled sequentially. The method may be advantageous when nutrient uptake in layers is calculated (this still needs verification). The distribution was adjusted: X-values were shifted half a Rad_AK to the right and Y-values half a Rad_AK upward (see slide Filling columns 2). CAVE: method not up to date and should be improved based on distributeHexagonRow (e.g., calculation of ErrorReg).

Writes coordinates and areas of PosArr to a file. With a structural model, output occurs at each time step; with static root positions only at the start of the model run. For testing in the static case, the positions of roots in the margins are also written.
Removes roots located in the margins from the Pos array
Calculates the grid cell indices for each root position
Calculates the number of valid/considered roots (located in the observation window but not in the margins).
Tests if roots are located within the border
Set and get methods for TRasterData
TRasterData Ende
Implementierung TSubmodRootDiff

Creates and initializes state variables, variables and parameters. The first parameter of the function call passes a string identical to the identifier and can be searched for. The second parameter contains a string indicating the unit used ([-] for dimensionless parameters, etc.). The third parameter is the actual floating-point value. For an explanation of the identifiers, see the declaration.

procedure.name: CalcRates
procedure.devnotes: there are three options for defining the location and number of roots rasterdatafile: root counts within a pre defined grid are input xyfile: a list of defined roots with their exact position input inppar: only the average root length density is defined, the root position are then calculated either in a regular hexagonal grid or as pure random positions Only for further definition ..

devnotes.summary: Method overridden to successively change the time step width.
Performs various initializations
Returns the RasterData instance

Declaration of class TRasterData. This class encapsulates the raster, positions and the number of read and randomized data (WAP). It enables display in the tabs RasterData and RootDistribution. The class also handles file access.
Published declarations
Property for accessing the TChart object in the Hume form
Property for accessing the TAdvStringGrid object in the Hume form
Property for accessing the TSubmodRootStrucNew object in the Hume form
Returns the calculated N amount [kgN/ha]; converts the existing concentration (aggregated values).
Returns the concentration [Mol N/cm^3]
